title: grevillea refracta subsp. refracta
intro: Grevillea refracta subsp. refracta is a plant species native to Western Australia, classified as a subspecies of the Grevillea refracta species. It is also known as the Western Australia or Western Spider Flower. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Grevillea refracta subsp. refracta include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are obtained through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
